Item 1. Business

The Company designs, manufactures and markets mobile communication and media devices,
personal computers, and portable digital music players, and sells a variety of related
software, services, accessories, networking solutions, and third-party digital content and
applications.

Products and services include smartphone hardware, personal computers, tablets, wearables
and accessories, and subscription-based Internet services including cloud and digital
distribution platforms.

For the fiscal year ended September 30, 2023, net sales by reportable segment were:
iPhone 52% of total net sales; Mac 10%; iPad 8%; Wearables, Home and Accessories 11%;
and Services 19%.

For the same period, net sales by region were: Americas 42%; Europe 24%;
Greater China 18%; Japan 7%; and Rest of Asia Pacific 9%. These percentages are
stated as approximate shares of total net sales as disclosed in management discussion.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis

Outlook for the next fiscal quarter
For the first fiscal quarter ending December 30, 2023, we expect total company revenue
to be in the range of $118 billion to $121 billion. Regarding our Services segment, we
expect year-over-year revenue growth in the mid-teens, consistent with our recent trends.

Industry and competitive environment
We believe global demand for premium smartphones has moderated compared to prior years
as customer upgrade cycles have lengthened. We further noted that competitive intensity
in our major product categories remains elevated across geographic regions.

Supplemental management remarks (same reporting period; attributable quotes below)
Luca Maestri, CFO, stated: "For the first fiscal quarter ending December 30, 2023, we expect total company revenue to be in the range of $118 billion to $121 billion."

Tim Cook, CEO, stated: "Regarding our Services segment, we expect year-over-year revenue growth in the mid-teens, consistent with our recent trends."

Tim Cook, CEO, stated: "We further noted that competitive intensity in our major product categories remains elevated across geographic regions."

Item 8. Recent corporate developments

During the third quarter of fiscal 2024, we launched a new paid tier of our cloud analytics
platform for enterprise customers, expanding our software subscription offerings.

On June 15, 2024, we completed the acquisition of DataSense Inc., a privately held provider of
on-device machine learning tools, for approximately $450 million in cash.

We entered into a strategic partnership with Orion Networks under which the parties will jointly
market our security software to small and medium-sized businesses in North America, as described
in the agreement filed as an exhibit to this report.
